2 || Attachment 1
3 1. Cross-Complainant University of San Francisco (“the University” or “Cross-
4 Complainant”) alleges as follows:

5 2. University of San Francisco is, and at all times herein relevant was, a not-for-
6 profit, public benefit corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of
7 California, with its principal place of business in the City and County of San Francisco,

8 California.

9 3. During the period June 1, 1999 to May 31, 2002, Cross-Defendant Patricia
10 Westerfield (“Cross-Defendant”) was an employee of the University, with all duties and
ae obligations to her employer, the University, as provided by law and the contractual terms and
12 conditions of her employment. Cross-Defendant was an employee employed by the University
13 in the department known as the University’s Disability Related Services Office (DRS). DRS
14 supports the University’s efforts to provide services to its undergraduate and graduate students
I5 who have physical, emotional, psychiatric and other mixed-medical disabilities, as defined by
16 law. The University has an extensive program to assess student requests for reasonable
M7 academic and other accommodations for such disabilities.

18 4. DRS handles highly confidential medical information about University students,
19 including psychiatric information about students, information about physical diseases and other
20 private medical facts. DRS assures students that their medical information will remain
21 confidential and will not leave the University. Without that assurance, students with disabilities
22 would feel reluctant to come to the DRS and the University could not carry out its duty to assist
23 them.

24 5. DRS and the University have strict policies against breach by University staff
25 members (such as Cross-Defendant) of the confidentiality of student medical information.
26 Extensive psychiatric, physical disease, emotional and other private and personal health and
a7 other information stored in DRS solely is to be used for providing services to those University
28

1 || students. Cross-Defendant was fully aware of these policies and rules and, agreed to abide by
2 || them, but she breached that agreement.

3 6. In or about March 2002, April 2002, and/or May 2002, Cross-Defendant secretly,
4 |\surreptiously, and without authorization from the University, recorded private medical
5 ||information of dozens of University students, copied their confidential medical documents,
6 |jmanufactured documents containing the names, social security numbers and medical conditions
7 || (psychiatric, physical, other) of such students, and then, in an effort to prevent the University
8 || and/or the students from discovering her actions, deleted from University computers, electronic
9 || documents containing evidence of these acts, and then permanently removed to her possession,
10 ||at her home and other locations to be discovered, such records, documents and information
11. || (herein “materials I’); and has thereafter at all times, failed and refused to return such materials
12 ||to the University and to provide an accounting to the University of all locations, disclosures and
13 || copies of such materials.

14 7. In or about March 2002, April 2002, and/or May 2002, Cross-Defendant secretly,
15 ||surreptiously, and without authorization from the University, accessed records, business
16 || documents and personnel information of the University, and then removed to her possession, at
17 || her home and other locations to be discovered, such information, and has thereafter at all times,
18 || failed and refused to return such information to the University and to provide an accounting to
19 ||the University of all locations, disclosures, originals and copies of such information (hereafter
20 || called “materials IT’) that have been in the possession of Cross-Defendant, her attorneys, agents
21 || and representatives.

22 8. Cross-Complainant will seek leave to amend as Cross-Defendants those other
23 |lindividuals, attomeys, agents and representatives of Cross-Defendant, who are in receipt,
24 || possession or control of the University materials I and II, to be discovered.

25 |

FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION

26 For Declaratory Relief

27 9. University of San Francisco hereby incorporates by reference Paragraphs 1
28 || through 8, above.

2 ||possession of the materials I and II; (b) that Cross-Defendant violated her obligations to the
3 || University and/or to the University’s disabled students by engaging in such acts described in
4 |\paragraphs 1 through 8; and (c) requiring Cross-Defendant, her attorneys, agents and
5 || representatives, to provide a written accounting, under oath, of the current and past locations of
6 || materials I and II, and as to which to whom Cross-Defendant has permitted other individuals to
7 || access and/or copy.
8 SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION
P For Prohibitory and Mandatory Injunctive Relief
11. University of San Francisco hereby incorporates by reference Paragraphs 1

"0 through 8, above.
M 12. University of San Francisco seeks an order of the Court: (a) requiring Cross-
2 Defendant, her attorneys, agents and representatives, immediately to provide a written
8 accounting, under oath, of all of the current and past locations of materials I and II, and as to
“ which Cross-Defendant has permitted other individuals to access and/or copy; (b) requiring
a Cross-Defendant, her attorneys, agents and representatives, immediately to return to the
8 University, accompanied by an inventory thereof executed under oath, the originals and all
"7 copies of materials I and II; and enjoining Cross-Defendant, her attorneys, agents and
representatives, from further disclosing any and all materials I and II.

FR-2. [_]} Intentional or Negligent Misrepresentation
a. Defendant made representations of material fact [(_] as stated in Attachment FR-2.a [_] as follows:

d. Defendant made the representations with the intent to defraud and induce plaintiff to act as described
in item FR-5. At the time plaintiff acted, plaintiff did not know the representations were false and believed
they were true. Plaintiff acted in justifiable reliance upon the truth of the representations.

facts and would not have taken the action if plaintiff had known the facts.
